threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn't stop at every stop. Local drivers have their own rhythm, trust me.
Last night on the 59, a woman argued passionately with a cardboard cutout of David Beckham. Brixton life is never dull.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid on a bike, juggling apples, while a dog was barking along to a saxophonist on the corner of Karl-Marx-Straße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst from a stand at 3 AM, then argue politics with the vendor, all while listening to the latest techno beats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: If they know to avoid 16th & Mission during rush hour for both BART delays and the panhandler circuit, they've got street cred.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the bus while balancing his lunch on his head, no one even blinked. Just another Tuesday in the TL.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Siesta' means everything shuts down. In Gràcia, it's more about long dinner hours and lively nights that keep the barrio awake.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they slow down to read every street sign like it’s a treasure map, while locals just glide on by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn’t stop at the ‘real’ bus stops. A true local knows to just wave them down.
Last night on the 63, a guy serenaded us with a full-on rendition of 'Wonderwall'. Everyone joined in by the time we hit the market. Classic Peckham vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a guy feeding pigeons while his toddler tried to catch them. That's when it hit me—this place is a circus, and I'm front row.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip a Berliner Weisse while listening to spontaneous poetry on the street. It's like a living art installation.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the exact time the BART delays start and end, like it’s part of their morning routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a guy in a chicken suit tried to board the bus at Civic Center, the driver just laughed and told him to take the next one instead.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the local corners like Plaça de la Virreina, where real life unfolds over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still call it "La Rambla" without the "de Catalunya" and look confused trying to find the right queue for 'xurros' at breakfast.
